5.1.3. Settings via MPPT Control display
The optional MPPT Control display can be used to configure solar charger settings, with the exception of advanced settings such
as RX and TX port settings. For information on how to do this see the MPPT Control manual.
The MPPT Control
5.2. All settings explained
This chapter lists all solar charger settings that are user-configurable and also explains how to update firmware of the solar
charger.
Do not change settings unless you know what they are and what the effect of changing these settings will
be. Incorrect settings may cause system problems including damage to batteries. When in doubt, seek advice
from an experienced Victron Energy installer, dealer or distributor.
MPPT solar charger manual

excerpts.query: operation daily use display menu controls operator
excerpts.text:
These anchor points will automatically adjust depending on length of the night as
this changes with the seasons.
Streetlight control
The solar charger controls the streetlight:
• Via the load output terminals.
• Via the TX port together with a VE.Direct TX digital output cable. Also see the TX port settings [27] chapter for more details.
The streetlight algorithm is always applied in conjunction with the settings as configured in the Load output
menu:
• If the streetlight is disabled, then the (virtual) load output is controlled only by the configuration as made in
the load output menu.
• If streetlight is enabled, then it's an AND function: the load output will be on when both the conditions made
in the Load output menu are satisfied as well as the streetlight settings. Otherwise, it's off.
Make sure that load output setting is set to "Always on" or to "BatteryLife". Do not set it to "Always off" as this
will result in the light being always off.
For more configurable voltage levels to force the light off, the other load output options can also be used.
MPPT solar charger manual
Page 24
 Configuration and settings

excerpts.text:
Note that setting "low temperature cut-off" is not needed for Victron Lithium Smart batteries or for Victron Super Pack batteries
with serial number HQ2040 and above. This setting is only needed for lithim batteries that are unable to block charging when the
temperature drops too low.
5.2.2. Load output settings
These settings are typically used to drive the load output.
The load output settings are can also be used to control the VE.Direct TX port [27] enabling it to operate a BatteryProtect, a
relay or another load-shedding device. For more information, see the chapter TX port settings [27].
Make sure that the jumper link [16] is removed before configuring the load output. The jumper link should
remain removed thereafter. If the jumper is not removed, and the TX port [27] is configured as “normal
communication” and/or the RX port [28] is configured as “load output config” (these are the default settings),
the jumper will override the VictronConnect load output settings.
Be aware that the load output behaviour will be different than expected when the streetlight setting [24] is
enabled (disabled by default).
MPPT solar charger manual
Page 22
 Configuration and settings

• At sunrise - the lighting will be adjusted before sunrise.
• Time before sunrise - at 1h 0m before sunrise, the following adjustment will be made:
• Dim level - full brilliance will be restored (100%).
5.2.4. TX port settings
The VE.Direct-TX port can be used to send a signal to an external device. For example, to send a PWM signal to dim a
streetlight.
Be aware that changing away from the default setting while a jumper link [16] is used, the jumper link will
need to be removed and the load output algorithm has to be configured via the VictronConnect load output
setting [22] instead.
To use the TX port, a VE.Direct TX digital output cable is needed.
The functionality of the TX port can be set at:
• Normal communication:
This is the default setting. Use this function when connecting to a GX device, a VE.Direct Bluetooth Smart dongle, or any other
device that needs to communicate with the solar charger via the VE.Direct port.
• Pulse every 0.01 kWh:
Use this function in combination with an energy meter.
The TX port will emit a pulse each time an additional 0.01kWh of energy has been harvested. The TX port is normally high and
will be driven low for approximately 250ms for every 0.0

5.1.2. Settings via the jumper link
If the load output algorithm is configured via VictronConnect or the MPPT Control display the jumper link is
not used and it needs to be removed. Configuration via the jumper link is only needed in case the load output
algorithm cannot be configured by any other means.
If the VE.Direct port is going to be used for communication with a GX device, MPPT display or other
equipment, the jumper needs to be removed, and cannot be used anymore for load output configuration. If this
is the case, configure the load output by using the VictronConnect App or a display. See chapter Load output
settings [22] or Settings via MPPT Control display [16] for more information.
The load output algorithm can be configured via a jumper link, located in the VE.Direct connector:
• If VictronConnect or the MPPT Control display is used to configure the load output algorithm, the jumper link is not used.
Remove the jumper.
• If the jumper link is used, see the table below for the load output algorithm belonging to each jumper placement. Should the
VictronConnect App or display be used at a later stage to change the load output setting, the jumper link needs to be removed
be

Note that these functionalities (other than the first functionality) do not disable the ability of the unit to communicate. What
happens is that the unit will automatically detect incoming data, and while data is being received, it will resume normal
communication. Once the data reception has been completed, it will automatically return to its configured TX function.
For more in-depth "developer style" information on the VE.Direct port see the Data communication with Victron Energy products
document.
5.2.5. RX port settings
The VE.Direct-RX port can be used to receive a signal from an external device. For example to switch the solar charger on (or off)
from a signal sent by a battery management system (BMS).
Be aware that changing away from the default setting while a jumper link [16] is used, the jumper link will
need to be removed and the load output algorithm has to be configured via the VictronConnect load output
setting [22] instead.

excerpts.text:
5.5. VE.Smart Networking
VE.Smart Networking allows a variety of products connected to the same network to share data via Bluetooth and is specially
designed for smaller systems that do not have a GX device installed.
When this product is part of a VE.Smart Network, it can receive data or communicate with the following devices:
• All SmartSolar solar chargers.
• All BlueSolar solar chargers connected to a VE.Direct Bluetooth Smart dongle.
• The Smart Battery Sense.
• A BMV or SmartShunt battery monitor equipped with Bluetooth (or VE.Direct Bluetooth Smart dongle) and an optional BMV
temperature sensor.
• Certain Smart AC chargers.
• The SUN inverter.
For the product compatibility list see the VE.Smart manual located on the VictronConnect app product page.
VE.Smart Networking can be used for:
• Temperature sensing - the measured battery temperature is used by the chargers in the network for temperature compensated
charging and in case of lithium battery for the low temperature cut off.
• Battery voltage sensing - the measured battery voltage is used by the chargers in the network to to compensate the charge
voltage should there be a voltage drop over the battery cables.
• Current se

excerpts.query: network communication MasterBus NMEA CZone VE.Direct Bluetooth remote panel app
excerpts.text:
There can only be one product in the network that transmits battery voltage and/or battery temperature. It is not possible to use a
battery monitor together with a Smart Battery Sense, or multiple of these devices.
For the network to be operational all networked devices must be within Bluetooth transmission distance of each other.
A maximum of 10 devices can be joined into a VE.Smart Network.
Some older devices might not support VE.Smart Networking. for more information see the Limitations chapter in the VE.Smart
Networking manual.
Setting up the network
When setting up the network, first set up the Smart Battery Sense or battery monitor, and then add one or more solar chargers or
AC chargers to the network.
All solar chargers and AC chargers need to have the same charge settings. The easiest way to do this is to use a preset battery
type or a saved used defined battery type. A warning #66 message will be shown if there is a difference between the devices
charge settings.
To set up a new network:
• Open the VictronConnect app.
• Select one of the devices that needs to become part of the new VE.Smart network.
• Navigate to the settings page by clicking the gear 
  symbol.
• click on

excerpts.text:
5.3. Updating firmware
The firmware can be checked and updated with the VictronConnect app.
The VictronConnect app might ask on the first connection to update the firmware. If this is the case, let it perform a firmware
update.
If it did not automatically update, check if the firmware is already up to date using the following procedure:
• Connect to the solar charger.
• Click on the settings symbol 
 .
• Click the options symbol 
 .
• Go to product info.
• Check if you are running the latest firmware and look for the text: “This is the latest version”.
• If the solar charger does not have the most up-to-date firmware, perform a firmware update.
5.4. Disabling and enabling Bluetooth
Bluetooth is by default enabled. It can be disabled or enabled via the VictronConnect app.
To disable or enable Bluetooth:
• Connect with the VictronConnect app to the solar charger. Note that if Bluetooth has been disabled, a connection via its built-in
Bluetooth is not possible anymore. In that case use the VE.Direct to USB interface, the VE.Direct Bluetooth Smart dongle or
VRM to connect to the solar charger.
• Select the solar charger from the VictronConnect list.
• Navigate to the solar charger sett

• Pre-defined factory battery presets.
• User-defined battery presets.
• Create, modify or delete a user-defined preset.
This setting uses factory pre-defined presets for a large variety of battery types. These pre-defined charge algorithms are suitable
for almost all installations.
It is possible to also create user-defined battery presets. The chapter Customize battery charge algorithm [18] explains how to
do this. These user-defined presets are stored in the VictronConnect app library. This is helpful in case multiple solar chargers
need to be configured, eliminating the need to define the entire charge algorithm each time a new solar charger is configured.
Expert mode
This setting enables or disables expert mode. It is by default set to "disabled".
The default charge algorithms work well for almost all installations. Only enable expert settings if your
equipment has special requirements.
When this setting is enabled the following parameters can be configured:
• Charger voltages: bulk, absorption and float.
• Bulk: re-bulk voltage offset.
• Absorption: duration, time and tail current.
• Equalization: current, interval, stop mode and duration.
• Temperature voltage compensation.

Solar charger system with inverter connected directly to the battery and controlled by the load output
Alternatively, a BatteryProtect can be used to control the load.
Solar charger system with DC loads connected directly to the battery via a BatteryProtect controlled by the load output
3.8.2. Virtual load output
A virtual load output can be established to control loads with current ratings greater than the solar charger's load output.
To create a virtual load output:
• Use the VE.Direct TX cable and enable it to act as a virtual load output via the VictronConnect App RX port function. Refer to
the RX port settings [28] chapter.
The virtual load output can be set up within the VictronConnect app and controlled using battery voltages or the BatteryLife
algorithm. For details on the configuration process, please consult the Load output settings [22] chapter.
3.8.3. BatteryLife
When the solar charger is not able to recharge the battery to its full capacity within one day, the result is often that the battery will
continually be cycled between a ‘partially charged’ state and the ‘end of discharge’ state. This mode of operation (no regular full
recharge) will destroy a lead-ac

excerpts.query: shore power AC input current limit generator mains
excerpts.text:
Note that the AES timer only runs when Vbat is below Vhigh, but is reset when Vbat goes above Vhigh.
For example: Let's say that the load is configured to turn on when the battery is at 14V (Vhigh) and to turn off
at 10V (Vlow):
• If the battery is kept above 14V, the load will never turn off, as the AES timer is continuously reset.
• If the battery is below 14V, the AES timer counts down and the load will turn off once the AES timer expires.
• If the battery drops below 10V, the load will turn off regardless of the state of the AES timer.
The "always off" and the "always on" modes will respond immediately. The other modes have a 2-minute delay before the load
output changes. This is so that the solar charger does not respond too quickly when, for example, an inrush current briefly lowers
the battery voltage below the threshold.
The streetlight [24] algorithm can also control the load output. The load output is off when the battery voltage is below the load
disconnect voltage or when streetlight dimming is at 0%. The load output turns back on when the battery voltage is above the
load reconnect voltage and streetlight dimming is between 1 and 100%.
5.2.3. Streetlight settings
The s

excerpts.text:
The chart below indicates the absorption and float charge voltage behaviour at different temperatures. The graph displays the
temperature compensation for a 12V system and uses a -16mV/°C temperature compensation coefficient. For a 48V system
multiply by 4.
Temperature compensated charge graph
By default, the solar charger uses its internal temperature for battery temperature compensated charging. An internal temperature
reading is taken in the morning and then again when the e solar charger has been idle for at least one hour, for example when the
charger is not actively charging a battery or supplying a load.
When the solar charger is part of VE.Smart Networking and receives a battery temperature reading from a Battery Sense or a
battery monitor with a temperature sensor, the actual battery temperature will be used for temperature compensated charging
throughout the day.
Low temperature cut-off
This setting is used to prevent damage to a lithium battery by disabling charging at low temperatures.
The "Low temperature cut-off" feature is only active when the solar charger is part of a VE.Smart network and
is receiving a battery temperature reading from a Battery Sense or a battery

excerpts.text:
5.2.1. Battery settings
Battery voltage
The battery voltage is automatically detected at the very first power-up of the solar charger and the battery voltage is set
accordingly. Further automatic detection is disabled. To make sure that a stable measurement is used, the charger first waits 10
seconds, and thereafter takes an averaged measurement. Note that the solar charger will remain off during this time.
In case the solar charger does not measure a battery voltage, it will default to 12V and store that. This will happen if the solar
charger is powered via its PV terminals, while not connected to a battery.
Note that the solar charger will not automatically detect a 36V battery. This will need to be set manually.
After automatic detection has taken place, the battery voltage can be changed and set to 12, 24, 36 or 48V, if so required (36V
and 48V setting is only possible for the 100/20 model).
Tip:
If the firmware of the solar charger needs to be updated, while keeping the automatic voltage detection active,
for example before shipping the unit to an end-user, do the following:
• Update the firmware.
• Once the firmware update is complete, go to the settings page in the VictronCo

assembled_user_prompt:
You extract an interaction profile for one piece of marine equipment from excerpts
of its official manual(s). You record observable facts about how humans interact
with the device. You do NOT judge importance or guide tier. You do NOT write guest
prose.

Rules:

1. Use ONLY the manual text provided. No general knowledge about this product or
   brand. Unmentioned = empty array / false / "unclear". Never guess.
   device.category_freeform must be the manual's own product category words
   (e.g. "charger/inverter", "MPPT solar charger") — never registry/taxonomy
   tokens such as electrical_dc, propulsion, freshwater_system.

2. Empty is meaningful. A busbar has no control surfaces and no operator actions;
   emit empty arrays.

3. audience per action, by the manual's own framing:
   - routine steps addressed to "you" → operator
   - "consult your dealer", "qualified personnel", installation-chapter procedures,
     DIP-switch / DIP table configuration → installer_or_technician
     (DIP configuration is always installer_or_technician + commissioning)
   - genuinely both → either

4. context per action: daily / situational / emergency / commissioning /
   maintenance. One procedure fitting two contexts → two entries.

5. Split actions: each action string is one verb phrase with one context.
   "monitor and configure via the app" → two actions (e.g. monitor → daily;
   configure → situational or commissioning per the manual's framing).
   Splitting bundled actions must never remove actions. Every distinct
   procedure the manual addresses to the operator must appear, including
   shutdown, restart, and fault/error-code procedures.
   has_emergency_procedure: true when the manual has an error-code / fault
   table or protective-disconnect recovery — not merely because an orderly
   service/storage shutdown exists (see calibration J).

6. control_surfaces on the base product vs. accessories sold separately: mark the
   latter optional_accessory: true. If a capability requires another product, also
   add a requires_devices entry with needed_for pointing at that field path
   (e.g. "control_surfaces[0]" or "data_roles.exposes_data_to_network").
   Never set needed_for to "" / null. Never attach requires_devices to a surface
   with optional_accessory: false except software_app (downloadable app for a
   built-in surface — record it with requirement_kind: software_app).
   Multiple requires_devices entries that share the same needed_for are OR
   alternatives (any one present on the vessel activates the capability);
   emit separate entries per alternative product — never stuff "A or B" into
   one description_verbatim when both are first-class options.
   requirement_kind (required on each entry):
     device — vessel line-item product (GX device, remote panel, safety relay)
     cable_or_consumable — cable/cord/dongle/consumable (VE.Direct cable)
     software_app — downloadable app (VictronConnect app)
     commissioning_tool — installer config/commissioning software
       (MasterAdjust, CZone Configuration Tool)

7. networks: record names verbatim in speaks (with physical_or_wireless when known).
   Ports/protocols that exist on the device belong in speaks even when their
   usefulness depends on another product — express that dependency with
   requires_devices, not by omitting the speak or zeroing the capability.
   Record a bridge ONLY if the manual explicitly describes translating/gatewaying
   between two named networks. Being connectable to a network is speaks, not bridges.

8. Multiple model variants in one manual: profile the requested model only. Ignore
   accessories and requirements that apply only to other variants. If the manual
   does not distinguish, say so in confidence.notes and lower confidence.overall.

9. evidence: 1-8 objects {supports_field, manual_section, note}. supports_field is
   the schema path justified. manual_section is a section title/heading (verbatim
   titles are expected). note is a <=12-word paraphrase — never copy manual
   sentences. Prefer coverage across consequential fields — not multiple evidence
   rows for one field. Fill in this priority order (stop when the cap is reached):
   (a) data_roles fields set true, and each requires_devices entry;
   (b) safety_role fields set true;
   (c) representative operator_actions (at least the emergency-context ones);
   (d) control_surfaces.
   Do not invent extra mandatory evidence beyond what this priority covers.

10. Optional protected_by / protects / supply_requirements: record when the
    manual explicitly states guard devices or supply needs — including in
    installer-framed installation chapters. audience applies to
    operator_actions only, never to requirement fields. Otherwise empty arrays.

11. safety_role keys are exactly: is_protective_device, has_manual_override,
    has_emergency_procedure. Do not invent other keys.

CALIBRATION — these examples concern OTHER devices / situations. Match the
field decisions (context, audience, optional_accessory, dependency shape).
Do NOT copy action strings or product-specific wording from the examples into
the device under extraction unless that wording appears in THIS device's
manual excerpts.

A. "To connect shore power: set the AC input current limit to match the dock
   supply using the rotary selector."
   → context: situational; audience: operator; one verb-phrase action naming
     the current-limit adjustment (word it from the manual under extraction).

B. "Battery type selection must be performed at commissioning. Consult your
   Balmar dealer for programming assistance."
   → context: commissioning; audience: installer_or_technician; one action for
     the battery-type selection (word it from the manual under extraction).

C. "If the BMS has disconnected the battery, remove the fault cause, then press
   and hold the reset button for 5 seconds."
   → context: emergency; audience: operator; one action describing that device's
     protective-disconnect recovery in the manual's own words (never paste a
     stock phrase from these examples).

D. "The charger can be monitored and configured via the VictronConnect app over
   Bluetooth."
   → control_surface: mobile_app_bluetooth, location_class: wireless,
     optional_accessory: false;
     PLUS two operator_actions: "monitor charger via VictronConnect app"
     (daily) and "configure charger via VictronConnect app" (situational or
     commissioning per framing) — never one combined "monitor and configure" action.

E. "With the optional Bluetooth Gateway, settings can be adjusted from the
   smartphone app."
   → control_surface: mobile_app_bluetooth, optional_accessory: true;
     plus requires_devices entry for the gateway with
     needed_for: "control_surfaces[0]".

F. "The unit connects to the NMEA 2000 backbone and transmits battery status PGNs."
   → networks.speaks: "NMEA 2000"; data_roles.exposes_data_to_network: true;
     bridges: EMPTY.

G. "The interface links the MasterBus network to CZone, allowing CZone displays
   to show MasterBus device data."
   → networks.bridges: [{from: "MasterBus", to: "CZone"}].

H. "Connect the unit's VE.Direct port to a GX device to monitor it remotely via VRM."
   → networks.speaks includes "VE.Direct" (wired);
     data_roles.exposes_data_to_network: true;
     requires_devices: {description_verbatim: "GX device",
     needed_for: "data_roles.exposes_data_to_network"}.
   Rationale: the port exists on the device → speaks; usefulness depending on
   another product → requires_devices, not by zeroing the capability or omitting
   the speak.

I. "BlueSolar models without built-in Bluetooth require the VE.Direct Bluetooth
   Smart Dongle." (when profiling a SmartSolar with built-in Bluetooth)
   → NO requires_devices entry for the dongle and NO control_surface change.
   Accessories that apply only to other model variants are ignored (see rule 8).

J. "To shut down the charger for service or storage: disconnect the PV supply,
   then the battery supply."
   → action: "shutdown the solar charger" (or equivalent); context: situational
   (or maintenance), NOT emergency. Prescribed orderly shutdown for service/
   storage is not an emergency procedure; fault response (error codes,
   protective disconnect recovery) is.

K. "The unit can be operated with the optional MasterView remote panel, installed
   at a convenient location."
   → control_surface: {surface: remote_panel_accessory, location_class:
     remote_wired, optional_accessory: true, label_verbatim from the manual};
     PLUS requires_devices: {description_verbatim: "MasterView remote panel"
     (or the manual's own panel name), needed_for: that surface's path}.
   Rationale: a separately sold panel/display that operates THIS device is a
   control surface of this device, not a different product.

L. "Install a fuse in the positive DC supply cable within 30cm of the battery."
   → supply_requirements entry describing that fuse mandate (word it from the
     manual under extraction — never paste this example sentence; include
     manual_section on the supporting evidence); also record protected_by when
     the manual frames the fuse as protecting this unit.
   Rationale: protection and supply mandates are FACTS to record even when the
   surrounding procedure is installer-audience — audience applies to
   operator_actions, never to requirement fields
   (supply_requirements / protected_by / protects).

M. (automatic protection + manual override — lithium/BMS style)
   Part 1 — Manual: "The safety relay will automatically open (REMOTE OFF) when
   built-in thresholds are met."
   → protects[] entry worded from the manual (safety relay automatically opens /
     protective disconnect on built-in thresholds). NOT an operator_action.
   Part 2 — Manual: "LOCK OFF knob position mechanically opens the safety relay"
     (or equivalent LOCK OFF / servicing isolation wording).
   → control_surfaces: {surface: rotary_selector (or physical_controls),
     location_class: on_device, optional_accessory: false, label from the manual};
     safety_role.has_manual_override: true.
   Operator recovery after a protective trip ("Close relay" once limits are OK)
   stays operator_actions with context: emergency — separate from Part 1/2.

N. (requirement_kind taxonomy — reuse known product names; set kind correctly)
   VictronConnect app → software_app;
   VE.Direct cable → cable_or_consumable;
   MasterAdjust / CZone Configuration Tool → commissioning_tool;
   GX device → device.
   Emit separate OR alternatives when the manual says "GX device or GlobalLink
   520" (two device entries, same needed_for) — never one combined description.

Return JSON only — no markdown wrapper — matching the interaction_profile schema.

These excerpts are one part of a larger manual; profile only what THIS text supports; empty fields are correct if this text doesn't cover them.
(map group: chapter_5)

DEVICE TO PROFILE:
{
  "manufacturer": "Victron Energy",
  "model": "SmartSolar MPPT 75 | 15",
  "category_freeform_hint": "Use the manual's own product category words only (never registry enums like electrical_dc)."
}

MANUAL SELECTION POLICY:
operators

SOURCE MANUALS:
[
  {

    "retrieval_score": 0.4648108144381087,
    "text": "Note that the AES timer only runs when Vbat is below Vhigh, but is reset when Vbat goes above Vhigh.\nFor example: Let's say that the load is configured to turn on when the battery is at 14V (Vhigh) and to turn off\nat 10V (Vlow):\n\u2022 If the battery is kept above 14V, the load will never turn off, as the AES timer is continuously reset.\n\u2022 If the battery is below 14V, the AES timer counts down and the load will turn off once the AES timer expires.\n\u2022 If the battery drops below 10V, the load will turn off regardless of the state of the AES timer.\nThe \"always off\" and the \"always on\" modes will respond immediately. The other modes have a 2-minute delay before the load\noutput changes. This is so that the solar charger does not respond too quickly when, for example, an inrush current briefly lowers\nthe battery voltage below the threshold.\nThe streetlight [24] algorithm can also control the load output. The load output is off when the battery voltage is below the load\ndisconnect voltage or when streetlight dimming is at 0%. The load output turns back on when the battery voltage is above the\nload reconnect voltage and streetlight dimming is between 1 and 100%.\n5.2.3. Streetlight settings\nThe s",

    "retrieval_phase": "heading_fill",
    "retrieval_score": 0.5173711919019774,
    "text": "5.2.1. Battery settings\nBattery voltage\nThe battery voltage is automatically detected at the very first power-up of the solar charger and the battery voltage is set\naccordingly. Further automatic detection is disabled. To make sure that a stable measurement is used, the charger first waits 10\nseconds, and thereafter takes an averaged measurement. Note that the solar charger will remain off during this time.\nIn case the solar charger does not measure a battery voltage, it will default to 12V and store that. This will happen if the solar\ncharger is powered via its PV terminals, while not connected to a battery.\nNote that the solar charger will not automatically detect a 36V battery. This will need to be set manually.\nAfter automatic detection has taken place, the battery voltage can be changed and set to 12, 24, 36 or 48V, if so required (36V\nand 48V setting is only possible for the 100/20 model).\nTip:\nIf the firmware of the solar charger needs to be updated, while keeping the automatic voltage detection active,\nfor example before shipping the unit to an end-user, do the following:\n\u2022 Update the firmware.\n\u2022 Once the firmware update is complete, go to the settings page in the VictronCo",
    "source_heading_guess": "5.2.1. Battery settings"
  }
]
